### Post by amauk on 2007-09-28
Beryl delegates the window frame generation to another program (called a window decorator)
no frame = decorator is not loaded

open up the terminal and type
emerald --replace

to solve the problem, you can either
add the above command to your startup programs (under System > Preferences > Session)
Or
There's an option in Beryl (somewhere, but I forget where) that will execute a decorator if Beryl detects there isn't one loaded

I'd recommend adding it to sessions, though
as this keeps all the startup programs in one place

---

### Post by Rupertronco on 2007-09-28
Add this to your screen options: ```
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True" 
```

Make sure to restart X after you do this (Ctrl + Alt + Backspace)

---
